steps.label: Definition
steps.content: A Dedekind cut is a partition of $\mathbb{Q}$ into two sets $A$ and $B$.
steps.narration: A Dedekind cut is a partition of the rationals into two sets A and B.
Here we see the number line split at the square root of two. All rationals less than root two go into A, and all rationals greater go into B.
A is the set of all rationals less than root two, and B is all rationals greater than or equal to root two.
Suppose we could list all real numbers as r one, r two, r three, and so on.
